# Bandwidth Control Policy Example

**Source:** https://help.zscaler.com/zia/bandwidth-control-policy-example
**Captured:** 2026-04-24 via Playwright MCP.

---

The bandwidth control policy specifies how an organization's bandwidth is allocated, which is relevant during periods of **contention**.

**Contention** occurs when bandwidth is saturated between the location and Zscaler. This typically happens at predictable periods on any given weekday — for example, bandwidth issues between 8:00 AM and 10:00 AM on a Monday are likely to occur every Monday or even every day of the week. These are episodic aspects of traffic management; it helps to observe traffic patterns for a while and then create **time-based policies** to address these contention periods.

For configuration, see *Configuring the Bandwidth Control Policy*. For how this policy fits into the overall enforcement order, see *About Policy Enforcement*.

(Sample policies — Example 1 and Example 2 — are referenced but not reproduced in this capture.)
